essence: Uses atmospheric models to predict pollution behaviour, assess environmental impact, and support regulatory decisions.

identity.title: Air Quality Modeller
identity.summary: Builds and runs atmospheric dispersion models, analyses emissions data, forecasts pollution levels, and supports environmental planning and regulation.

salary.notes: Modellers specialising in advanced dispersion systems, industrial permitting, or national‑scale forecasting can exceed £80k-£100k+.

qualifications: Training in atmospheric science, environmental science, physics, or data modelling
Knowledge of air‑quality regulations and emissions sources
Ability to use dispersion‑modelling software and analyse large datasets
Understanding of meteorology, pollution behaviour, and environmental impact assessment

lifestyle.description: Air quality modellers work for environmental consultancies, government agencies, research organisations, and infrastructure planners. They run atmospheric models, analyse emissions data, forecast pollution, and support environmental impact assessments. The role suits individuals who enjoy modelling, data analysis, and environmental protection.

habits: Analyses emissions inventories and meteorological data
Forecasts pollution levels and air‑quality trends
Supports environmental impact assessments and planning
Prepares technical reports for regulators and clients
